reportText: @ Canyon Mouth , 62 cfs @ Fort Collins , 2 c fs @ North Fork below Seaman Reservoir With runoff conditions stabilizing, water clarity on the Poudre has been excellent. The river has been slightly off-color in the mornings, but it has been clearing quickly as the day progresses. As summertime temperatures settle in, the higher sections of the canyon have seen an increase in bug activity, and the fishing has picked up considerably. The clearer water has made fish much more willing to rise to smaller caddis and stonefly dry flies. Green Drakes have also started to make an appearance, although they haven't been hatching in large numbers its a good idea to have a couple with you if you do run into them. Dry-dropper rigs have also been a productive approach, with foam stonefly dries on top and caddis, PMD, green drake or stonefly droppers below. Streamers continue to be a great option on the Poudre as well, with small baitfish and bugger pattens producing the most consistent results. If you have any questions or are interested in booking a guided trip on the Poudre, feel free to give either shop a call. We'll do our best to get you set up for a great day on the water. Take a look at our Poudre Assortment , a great selection for this time of year. Or, click the images below for a full list of our current recommended fly patterns.
